text.title: Keyboard-Only Navigation Testing
text.summary: Rules and checklists for keyboard-only navigation: tab order, focus indicators, interactive element behavior, and common failure patterns.

rule.statement: Every interactive element must be reachable and operable using only Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, Space, Escape, and Arrow keys — no mouse required.
rule.rationale: Screen reader users navigate by keyboard; motor-disability users may not use a mouse; legal requirement under WCAG 2.1 SC 2.1.1.

example.code: Tab: move to next focusable element Shift+Tab: move to previous focusable element Enter: activate link or button Space: activate button / toggle checkbox Arrow keys: navigate dropdown options, radio groups, tabs, sliders Escape: close modal / cancel action

rule.statement: Never remove the focus outline without providing a visible replacement with at least 3:1 contrast ratio.
rule.rationale: WCAG 2.2 SC 2.4.11 (AA): focused element must be at least partially visible. SC 2.4.13 (AAA): 2px minimum thickness, sufficient contrast.

antipattern.description: CSS rule outline: none or outline: 0 applied globally without a replacement focus style.
antipattern.reason: Keyboard users cannot see where they are; fails WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.11.

rule.statement: Custom interactive components must implement the keyboard interaction pattern specified in the ARIA Authoring Practices Guide (APG).
rule.rationale: Arbitrary implementations break AT user expectations built over years of using native widgets.

example.code: Accordion: Enter/Space to expand/collapse; Tab to next control Tabs: Arrow keys to switch tabs; Tab to move into tab panel Modal: Tab cycles within modal; Escape closes; focus returns to trigger Carousel: Arrow keys for prev/next; focus managed per panel Dropdown: Enter/Space to open; Arrow keys to navigate; Escape to close Slider: Arrow keys to adjust value; Home/End for min/max

antipattern.description: Drag-and-drop with no keyboard alternative (e.g., reorderable list, Kanban board).
antipattern.reason: Fails WCAG 2.5.7 (AA, new in 2.2). Must provide button-driven move alternatives for every drag interaction.

list.item: No visible focus — outline removed without replacement.
Illogical tab order — DOM order differs from visual layout; wrong tabindex values.
Keyboard trap — cannot Tab out of a widget or modal.
Mouse-only hover menus — no keyboard trigger to open navigation flyouts.
Focus lost after modal close — not returned to the trigger element.
Enter/Space not handled on custom div/span buttons.
